competitions.product: WIN A NEO-VINTAGE ROLEX SUBMARINER 14060 W/ BOX & BOOKLETS
competitions.product_info: Home – Rolex Submariner 14060 “No Date” WIN A NEO-VINTAGE ROLEX SUBMARINER 14060 W/ BOX & BOOKLETS Value: £6,500 Duration: 7 Days Age: Circa 1997 Max. Entries: 649 Ticket Price: £20 Live Draw: 16/06/26 The Rolex Submariner is one of the most iconic and popular wristwatches of all time , and this, the “no date Sub”, is the purest expression of that. This reference, the 14060, is the perfect marriage of classic vintage styling with modern durability and wearability – the perfect neo-vintage sports watch for all occasions. This U serial example dates to 1997 and is in very good condition , accompanied by its box, booklets, tag, and calendar card. 31.43% SOLD 0 Tickets Sold: 204 649 31.43% 445 Tickets remaining 445/649 Tickets Remaining Time Left to End: 5 Days 17 Hours 27 Minutes 13 Seconds ENTER NOW WIN A ROLEX SUBMARINER 14060 "NO DATE" 1997 ENTER NOW 31.43% SOLD 0 Tickets Sold: 204 649 31.43% 445 Tickets remaining 445/649 Tickets Remaining Time Left to End: 5 Days 17 Hours 27 Minutes 13 Seconds Value: £6,500 Duration: 7 Days Age: Circa 1997 Entries: 649 Max. Ticket Price: £20 Live Draw: 16/06/2026 The Rolex Submariner is one of the most iconic and popular wristwatches of all time , and this, the “no date Sub”, is the purest expression of that. This reference, the 14060, is the perfect marriage of classic vintage styling with modern durability and wearability – the perfect neo-vintage sports watch for all occasions. This U serial example dates to 1997 and is in very good condition , accompanied by its box, booklets, tag, and calendar card. #28 - The Rolex Submariner 14060 "No Date" w/ Box & Booklets 1997 The History— The Example The Rolex Submariner is one of the most iconic wristwatches ever created, and for many enthusiasts, the no-date Submariner is the model in its purest form. Free from the date window and Cyclops magnifier, the reference 14060 offers the clean, symmetrical dial layout that made the Submariner famous. Introduced in 1990, the 14060 occupies a particularly appealing place in the Submariner lineage, combining classic proportions and vintage charm with the durability and practicality expected of a modern Rolex. It remains one of the most wearable sports watches the brand has ever produced. This U serial example dates to 1997 and remains in very good condition . Sitting firmly within the tritium era, it retains the character and warmth that collectors look for in a neo-vintage Rolex, while still being a watch that can be enjoyed every day without compromise. Accompanying the watch are its box, booklets, tag and calendar card , making it a strong and increasingly collectible example of one of Rolex’s most enduring references. Overall, a versatile, understated and highly capable sports watch – the 14060 continues to be one of the smartest entries into vintage Rolex ownership. ENTER NOW The First Competition of its Kind The Vintage Watch Draw is unique. We’re the first competition website dedicated to collector-grade vintage watches, backed by a trusted expert. After the initial idea over five years ago, we’re so excited to now bring this new concept to you. Curated by A Trusted Expert Our competitions are curated by Alex Stevens, a trusted & reputable vintage watch specialist with lifelong experience in the trade. 12 Month Service Warranty Every watch is fully serviced by our watchmaker & comes with a 12 month mechanical warranty, so you can enjoy your watch hassle-free. Collector-Grade Condition Every watch is meticulously curated by Alex, known for selecting only the finest, collector-grade examples in terms of condition & originality. Low & Transparent Odds We don’t flood the draw with tickets. Our competitions are deliberately limited to keep odds low & your chance of winning fair. Authenticity Guarantee Every watch is thoroughly inspected & fully authenticated by our in-house expert. We are professionals, not enthusiasts or hobbyists. Guaranteed Draws Every draw ends on the stated date, no matter what. We never extend our competitions, even if we haven't sold all the tickets.
